plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-551 RP18.P00.M00.S01-RP18.P01.M02.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-551 bootstraps RP18 AI Legal Workflows as a descriptor-only program package: the full P00 Contract And Acceptance Baseline phase (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice, Secondary Workflow Slice, Permission And Audit Binding, Synthetic Fixture Set, Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles) and the P01 head (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, and the Type And Shape Definition head) as fourteen descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-552 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary:
Independent read-only review (Read/Grep/Glob only) of CP00-551 "RP18 AI Legal Workflows Core" descriptor-only bootstrap. VERDICT: PASS, no findings, does not block pack or goal closeout.

Coverage verified against docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json and the review baseline: exactly 150 RP18 units spanning RP18.P00.M00.S01–RP18.P01.M02.S08, all program_id=RP18, no duplicate IDs. Deliverable distribution reconciles to 150 (implementation 108, contract 8, security_audit 16, hermes_evidence 7, claude_review 3, ui 8) and micro-title counts reconcile to 150 across the 14 descriptor sections (P00 M00–M10 scope-foundation cycle + P01 M00–M02 model-foundation head). Section row titles match required_section_rows for every micro-phase in packages/ai-legal-workflows/src/registry.js.

Descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries hold. Every generated row carries descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false (service.js). The NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and the result freeze in registry.js/service.js set all runtime, permission-evaluation, audit-write, DB, object-storage, command-runtime, Hermes-runtime-receipt, and real-fixture flags to false, with synthetic_only/no_real_data true and the seven no-leak guards present. The src modules contain no filesystem writes, network, or DB I/O; the test and validator only Read files and assert. contracts/ai-legal-workflows-core-contract.json embeds program, current_pack, both attestation copies, the full scope_contract_foundation_descriptor (row-extras match service ROW_EXTRAS), cp551_requirements, and historical_pack_bindings ending at CP00-551 — consistent with the validator's deepEqual drift checks.

Authority boundary respected: descriptor.authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, claude_packet.read_only=true with allowed_tools=[Read,Grep,Glob]. Production posture is correctly conservative — manifest.json reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer="descriptor", status="implemented_pending_review"; the production_ready_flag is descriptor-verification only, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim. Hermes is used solely for a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime.

Review-hardening (CP178+) checks pass: readiness-report.json auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte well-formed JSON stdout, 0 stderr); no forbidden evidence patterns (no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, or missing fields); review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved=[] for this pack. Wiring confirmed: package.json registers rp18:ai-legal-workflows-core:validate and npm test includes packages/ai-legal-workflows/test/*.test.js. Scope hygiene confirmed: unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, .docx) are correctly excluded per changed-file-scope.json and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[]. Note: npm run validate (validate-product-contract.mjs) validates at the module level (legal-knowledge-ai) and does not enumerate individual RP programs, so RP18's absence there is by-design, not a gap. Claude is acting only as a read-only independent reviewer; final approval and the enterprise-trust claim remain a human responsibility outside this receipt.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-551 ai legal workflows core scope contract foundation descriptor pack
